content.closing1:
Tea Farm Walks & Tea Experiences in Tigoni

Tigoni is the birthplace of KenyaвҖҷs tea industry, with tea first grown here experimentally in 1903 and the first commercial estate established in 1910. Walking through these estates is both scenic and deeply historic.

Ravenswood Farm offers serene walks through expansive tea fields and indigenous trees, making it ideal for slow nature walks, photography, and dog-friendly strolls. The quiet paths and elevated views give visitors a true sense of TigoniвҖҷs agricultural heritage.

Kiambethu Tea Farm, one of KenyaвҖҷs oldest tea farms, provides guided tea experiences that combine plantation walks, tea education, and tastings. Visitors learn how tea is grown, harvested, and processed while enjoying panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

Gathoni Tea Farm is known for its purple tea and small-scale, sustainable approach. Tours here feel personal and educational, offering insight into modern tea innovation while remaining rooted in TigoniвҖҷs traditions.

Little Falls Tea Garden combines tea gardens with riverside scenery and gentle walking trails, making it a peaceful option for visitors looking to blend nature, water features, and tea landscapes.

eBike Tours in Tigoni

One of the most exciting and accessible ways to explore Tigoni is through a guided eBike tour. With the help of electric-assisted bicycles, visitors can cover more ground comfortably while still enjoying the fresh air, scenic views, and quiet countryside that define the region. eBike tours are suitable for all fitness levels, making them ideal for beginners, families with older children, couples, and groups.

eBike routes in Tigoni wind through rolling tea plantations, forest paths, rural backroads, and scenic viewpoints, offering a deeper look into the landscape beyond what you can see by car. The electric boost makes it easy to tackle TigoniвҖҷs hills, allowing riders to focus on the experience rather than the effort.

Nature Trails & Outdoor Walks

TigoniвҖҷs elevationвҖ”approximately 2,100 metres above sea levelвҖ”creates a cool climate and lush environment that is perfect for walking, hiking, and trail exploration.

The Brackenhurst Forest Trail is one of the most popular nature walks in the area. It winds through indigenous forest, offering shaded paths, birdsong, and a tranquil atmosphere ideal for nature lovers and beginner hikers.

The Ravenswood Trail runs alongside tea fields and forest edges, offering open views and gentle terrain. It is especially popular for relaxed hikes, dog walks, and sunset strolls.

Food & CafГ©s in Tigoni

Tigoni has quietly become one of KenyaвҖҷs most exciting countryside food destinations, offering everything from farm-to-table cafГ©s to refined fusion dining.

The Fig & Olive Restaurant. This iconic Tigoni Cafe is loved for its relaxed elegance, dog-friendly policy, and family appeal. Its childrenвҖҷs section, Amuse, allows kids to paint and create while adults enjoy long lunches by the fireplace or in the garden.

Como offers high-end Kenyan fusion cuisine in a stylish outdoor setting. Created by Swiss-trained chef Stephanie Kiragu, it is ideal for special lunches, refined dinners, and culinary-focused visitors.

Wellness & Spas

For visitors seeking rest and restoration, Tigoni offers a slower, more intentional approach to wellness.

Serenity Spa Tigoni is tucked away in the highlands, offering massages, spa treatments, and wellness experiences surrounded by nature. It is an ideal way to unwind after hiking, cycling, or a full day of exploration.

Why Visit Tigoni?

Tigoni is not a town in the traditional senseвҖ”it is a region, shaped by tea, history, landscape, and community. Its cool climate, rolling hills, and understated luxury make it one of KenyaвҖҷs most rewarding destinations for slow travel, nature, food, and wellness.

Whether youвҖҷre planning a day trip, a romantic escape, a family outing, or a curated multi-activity itinerary, Tigoni offers depth, beauty, and calmвҖ”just a short drive from Nairobi.

content.subheading: Ravenswood Waterfall
content.intro: Beyond its rolling tea plantations and cool highland air, Tigoni is home to a series of quiet, lesser-known waterfalls tucked within forests, tea estates, and countryside trails. These waterfalls are not large or commercialized, but that is precisely their charm. They are peaceful, scenic, and deeply connected to the landscape—best experienced slowly, on foot, and with local knowledge.
paragraphs: Beyond its rolling tea plantations and cool highland air, Tigoni is home to a series of quiet, lesser-known waterfalls tucked within forests, tea estates, and countryside trails. These waterfalls are not large or commercialized, but that is precisely their charm. They are peaceful, scenic, and deeply connected to the landscapeвҖ”best experienced slowly, on foot, and with local knowledge.
closing: Here are the most notable waterfalls to explore in Tigoni.
Ravenswood Waterfall

The Ravenswood Waterfall is one of TigoniвҖҷs most serene natural features, hidden within the expansive Ravenswood tea estate. Reached via gentle walking trails that pass through tea fields and forest edges, this waterfall is ideal for visitors seeking calm and privacy.

The surrounding area is lush and shaded, making it a popular stop during nature walks and guided hikes. The sound of flowing water, combined with the open tea landscape, creates a peaceful atmosphere perfect for photography, quiet reflection, or a short rest during a longer walk. It is also part of a dog-friendly trail, making it suitable for visitors exploring with pets.
Kawamwaki (Kwamwaki) Waterfall

The Kawamwaki Waterfall, also commonly referred to as Kwamwaki Waterfall, is one of TigoniвҖҷs more adventurous waterfall spots. Reaching it involves walking through rural trails and sections of forest, giving visitors a more immersive countryside experience.

This waterfall feels raw and natural, surrounded by indigenous vegetation and open farmland. It is best explored with a local guide, especially after rainy seasons when paths can be slippery. For hikers and nature lovers looking for something off the beaten path, Kawamwaki offers a rewarding and authentic Tigoni experience.
Tigoni Tea Estate Waterfalls

Scattered within the Tigoni Tea Estate are small, seasonal waterfalls formed by natural streams cutting through the tea fields. These waterfalls are most active during the rainy months, when water flows steadily through the estate.

Visiting these waterfalls is often part of a tea estate walk, where guests experience both the agricultural landscape and its hidden natural features. The contrast between manicured tea rows and flowing water creates a striking visual experience unique to Tigoni.
Little Falls Tea Garden Waterfall

The Little Falls Tea Farm Waterfall lives up to its nameвҖ”a small, charming waterfall set within a peaceful tea garden environment. It is easy to access and well suited for relaxed walks, families, and visitors looking for a gentle nature experience rather than a strenuous hike.

Surrounded by tea bushes and greenery, the waterfall adds a calming focal point to the farm walk and is especially picturesque after rainfall. ItвҖҷs an ideal stop for casual explorers, photographers, and anyone seeking a quiet moment in nature.
Tips for Exploring Tigoni Waterfalls

Wear sturdy walking shoes, especially during rainy seasons. Consider guided walks for waterfalls like Kawamwaki, as trails can be less defined. Respect private tea estates by visiting only with permission or through organized tours, and always carry out what you bring in to preserve TigoniвҖҷs natural beauty.

TigoniвҖҷs appeal as a picnic destination goes beyond individual venues. Planning Your Picnic in Tigoni
When planning a picnic in Tigoni, it is advisable to:

Experience Tigoni the Local Way

Picnicking in Tigoni is more than just an outdoor meal вҖ” it is a chance to experience the landscape, lifestyle, and calm that define this highland region. From riverside adventures and waterfall views to exclusive retreats and garden picnics, Tigoni continues to emerge as one of the most rewarding countryside escapes near Nairobi.

gridPosts.title: THE 7 BEST Nairobi Hiking Trails (2026)

Discover the breathtaking Tigoni & Limuru Highlands, offering scenic routes through rolling tea plantations, indigenous forests, and serene waterfalls. paragraphs: Top Hiking Trails in Tigoni & Limuru Highlands

Just under an hour from Nairobi, Tigoni and the Limuru highlands offer some of the most rewarding hiking trails in Central Kenya. Known for rolling tea plantations, cool weather, indigenous forests, dams, and waterfalls, Tigoni is ideal for both beginners and experienced hikers looking for scenic, low-altitude escapes in 2026.
Below are the best Tigoni hiking trails, regularly used by local hiking groups and outdoor operators.
closing: рҹҘҫ Top Tigoni Hiking Trails
1. Ravenswood Walk
One of TigoniвҖҷs most scenic and balanced hikes, set within a working tea estate.

• Duration: ~2 hours
  • Terrain: Tea fields, forest paths, gentle ridges
  • Why itвҖҷs special: Expansive views, cool forest sections, and quiet plantation trails
2. Brackenhurst Walk
A longer, immersive trail through tea plantations and indigenous forest.

• Duration: ~4 hours
  • Terrain: Tea fields, forest trails, open slopes
  • Highlights: Birdlife, forest canopy, panoramic highland views
3. NiftyвҖҷs Escape (Nifty Loop)
A popular beginner-friendly hike starting and ending at NiftyвҖҷs Lounge.

• Duration: ~3 hours
  • Terrain: Plantations, dirt tracks, woodland paths
  • Ideal for: First-time hikers, social group walks
4. Limuru Country Club Walk
A relaxed, scenic walk around the historic golf course grounds.

• Distance: ~5 km
  • Terrain: Bamboo forest, dams, ponds, tea hedges
  • Bonus: Optional lunch or drinks at the clubhouse
5. Tigoni Tea Estate Trail
A classic Tigoni route winding through multiple tea zones.

• Features: Dams, waterfalls, man-made forests
  • Accessibility: Easily reachable from Nairobi
  • Best for: Photography and nature walks
6. Kawamwaki Trail
One of the most loved family-friendly hikes in Tigoni, often paired with lunch.

• Terrain: Farmland paths, forest, dam, and waterfalls
  • Difficulty: Easy to moderate
  • Popular stop: Routes often end near COMO Restaurant Tigoni
  • Why hikers love it: Waterfalls, open views, gentle gradients
7. Cianda Walk
A quieter countryside hike through the CiandaвҖ“Limuru area, offering authentic rural scenery.

• Terrain: Village paths, tea edges, small forests
  • Experience: Cultural, calm, and less commercial
  • Best for: Slow-paced walks and nature immersion
рҹҢҝ What to Expect When Hiking in Tigoni

• Scenery: Rolling tea plantations, lush forests, waterfalls, dams, and valley views
  • Wildlife: Frequent bird sightings and occasional colobus monkeys
  • Weather: Cool highland climate (around 2,100вҖ“2,300m altitude)
  • Trail Variety: Easy nature walks to longer endurance hikes
рҹ§ӯ Tips for Hiking Tigoni in 2026

• Go Guided: Local organizers like Lets Drift and Tigoni-based groups often arrange safe, mapped routes
  • Weather Watch: Trails can be slippery during rainy seasons
  • Footwear: Waterproof hiking shoes recommended
  • Timing: Morning hikes offer clearer views and cooler temperatures
Why Tigoni is One of NairobiвҖҷs Best Hiking Destinations
Tigoni combines accessibility, scenery, safety, and varietyвҖ”making it ideal for weekend hikers, families, photographers, and wellness seekers looking to reconnect with nature just outside Nairobi.

gridPosts.title: THE 15 BEST Things to Do in Nairobi (2026): Discover TigoniвҖҷs Highland Escape

content.subheading: THE 15 BEST Things to Do in Nairobi (2026): Discover TigoniвҖҷs Highland Escape
paragraphs: When Nairobi feels rushed and noisy, Tigoni offers a gentle reset. Perched in the cool highlands of Limuru and framed by endless tea plantations, forests, and valleys, Tigoni is where nature, culture, and slow living meetвҖ”just under an hour from the city. Whether youвҖҷre seeking adventure, wellness, or a quiet afternoon among tea fields, Tigoni delivers experiences that feel both grounding and unforgettable.
Here are 15 of the best things to do in Tigoni in 2026.
closing: 1. Kiambethu Tea Farm Tour

A visit to Kiambethu Farm is a journey into the roots of Kenyan tea. Established in 1910, this family-run estate offers guided walks through lush tea fields and an indigenous forest inhabited by colobus monkeys. Guests enjoy an educational tea talk, hands-on tea picking, and a generous three-course farm-to-table lunch served with sweeping views of the Ngong Hills. ItвҖҷs one of the most authentic day trips from Nairobi.
2. Hike the Brackenhurst Forest Trails

The Brackenhurst Walk winds through tea plantations, forest paths, and open hillsides, offering cool shade, birdsong, and wide views. ItвҖҷs one of TigoniвҖҷs most scenic hikesвҖ”ideal for nature lovers and photographers. The terrain is gentle enough for beginners but long enough to feel rewarding.
3. Explore Tigoni on a Guided E-Bike Tour

For a modern twist on countryside exploration, guided e-bike tours let you glide effortlessly through rolling hills, tea estates, and forest trails. With routes suitable for all fitness levels, these rides combine adventure with storytelling, photo stops, and local insights youвҖҷd otherwise miss.
4. Experience Classic Country Living at Kentmere Club

Kentmere Club is a slice of old-world charm hidden in TigoniвҖҷs greenery. Visitors can enjoy golf, tennis, horse riding, or a relaxed lunch overlooking manicured lawns. ItвҖҷs perfect for those who appreciate heritage, leisure, and quiet elegance.
5. Unwind at Serenity Spa

Tucked within TigoniвҖҷs calm landscape, Serenity Spa lives up to its name. From massages to wellness therapies and occasional yoga sessions, itвҖҷs a sanctuary designed for deep relaxationвҖ”best enjoyed after a hike or long week in the city.
6. Visit Tigoni Waterfalls & Dam

A short hike leads to TigoniвҖҷs hidden waterfalls and dam, where flowing water, forest cover, and open skies create a peaceful retreat. ItвҖҷs a favorite for picnics, light hikes, and sunset photography with distant views of Nairobi.
7. Discover Sustainable Living at Mlango Farm

Mlango Farm blends organic farming with education and dining. Guided tours introduce visitors to sustainable agriculture, animal care, and harvesting, followed by a fresh farm-to-table lunch. ItвҖҷs an enriching experience for families, food lovers, and anyone curious about slow, intentional living.
8. Go Horse Riding Through Tea Plantations

Few experiences rival horse riding in Tigoni. Guided trails take riders through tea estates, open fields, and forest paths, offering a calm yet adventurous way to explore the countryside. Suitable for beginners and experienced riders alike.
9. Explore Local Art at Banana Hill & Red Hill Galleries

content.subheading: Kiambethu Farm: Historic Tea Estate & Immersive Tea Tour in Tigoni
paragraphs: Kiambethu Farm is a historic, familyвҖ‘run tea plantation located in Limuru, near Tigoni, Kenya. Just about 30вҖҜkm northwest of NairobiвҖҷs city centre, it offers visitors a tranquil countryside escape and a unique chance to learn about KenyaвҖҷs tea industry at one of its earliest commercial estates.
For many travellers, Kiambethu Farm is more than a tour вҖ” itвҖҷs an immersive cultural experience combining tea education, nature walks, and farmвҖ‘fresh cuisine in a serene highland setting.
closing: Visitor Information

• Location: Girls School Road, Tigoni, Limuru Town, Kiambu County, Kenya.
  • Distance from Nairobi: Approximately 30вҖҜkm (18.6вҖҜmi) northwest of Nairobi.
  • Hours: Tours are available Thursday to Sunday, with tours starting at 11:00вҖҜAM. The farm is closed Mondays to Wednesdays.
  • Booking: Advance booking is essential as slots are limited. Book through the Kiambethu Farm website or via trusted tour operators.
  • Cost: Around KSh 4,700 per adult for the allвҖ‘inclusive tour and lunch (children under 12 are half price). Fees may vary slightly over time.
What to Expect on Your Visit
рҹҚө Tea History & Storytelling

Your day begins with a warm welcome вҖ” often with freshly brewed tea or coffee вҖ” as you hear an informal talk on the history of the farm and KenyaвҖҷs tea industry, often shared by the current host, Fiona Vernon, granddaughter of the founder.
рҹҢұ Farm & Forest Walk

A guided walk through the tea fields introduces you to the rhythms of plantation life. YouвҖҷll learn how tea is grown, what makes Kenyan tea unique, and how leaf selection impacts flavour. Follow this with a stroll through a protected indigenous forest, where guides share insights about local plants, birds, and wildlife вҖ” including the resident Colobus monkeys.
рҹҚҪпёҸ FarmвҖ‘toвҖ‘Table Lunch

Lunch is served around 1:00вҖҜPM as a threeвҖ‘course buffet created with fresh ingredients from the farmвҖҷs own garden. Expect crisp salads, hearty mains, and indulgent desserts вҖ” including homemade ice cream made with cream from the farmвҖҷs Channel Island cows. A preвҖ‘lunch drink on the veranda offers sweeping views across tea terraces toward the Ngong Hills.
вҳ• Tea & Biscuits
Throughout your visit, enjoy unlimited freshly brewed tea and homemade biscuits, perfectly complementing the tranquil garden atmosphere.
Why Kiambethu Farm Is Special
Kiambethu is not just a tea tour вҖ” itвҖҷs a multiвҖ‘sensory experience rooted in over a century of family history:

• It was first planted by A.B. McDonell in 1910, making it one of KenyaвҖҷs earliest commercial tea farms.
  • The farm remains familyвҖ‘run, offering personal narration and storytelling you wonвҖҷt find in large commercial attractions.
  • The combination of tea education, forest exploration, and farm cuisine makes it ideal for travellers seeking culture, nature, and gastronomy in one visit.
  • Reviewer feedback consistently highlights warm hospitality, informative guides, and delicious food as standout features.
Practical Tips for Your Visit

• Book Early: Tours can fill quickly, especially on weekends and holidays.
  • Arrive on Time: Tours start at 11:00вҖҜAM, and late arrivals may miss parts of the experience.
  • Comfortable Shoes: Bring comfortable walking shoes for field and forest paths.
  • Weather Ready: TigoniвҖҷs highland climate can be cool and misty вҖ” a light jacket is helpful.
  • Photography: This is a highly photogenic location вҖ” bring a camera for expansive tea vistas and wildlife moments.
A Highly Recommended Day Trip from Nairobi

Kiambethu Farm is widely regarded as one of the best tea farm experiences near Nairobi, combining education, nature, and culinary delight into a single visit. Whether youвҖҷre a tea enthusiast, a nature lover, or just looking for a peaceful escape into the Kenyan highlands, a visit here is both enriching and refreshing.
